mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate compression and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors price in long-term inflation expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns remain negative in 75%+ of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) signal deteriorating labor market conditions, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as risk appetite contracts.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates weakening supply chain capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations revise downward.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 70%+ of periods when INDPRO falls below 105.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) signal improved consumer confidence and business investment, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through direct demand-side stimulus.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 60%+ of periods when HOUST increases.

mechanism_narrative: A 5-month decline in real sales (RSAFS) indicates weakening consumer purchasing power, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as corporate revenue prospects deteriorate.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in 60%+ of periods when RSAFS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and decline in unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through wage inflation expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the federal funds rate above 4% signals aggressive monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through higher discount rates and reduced corporate borrowing.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 70%+ of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0.
